name: Phi Kappa Psi
summary: A national fraternity dedicated to the service of others.  Phi Psi provides leadership opportunities within the Chapter, the classroom, and in the community.  The Phi Kappa Psi Foundation supports members academically by keeping all members accountable.

name: Sigma Alpha Epsilon
summary: The mission of Sigma Alpha Epsilon is to promote the highest standards of friendship, scholarship, and service for our members based upon the ideals set forth by our Founders and as specifically enunciated in our creed.

name: Tau Kappa Epsilon
summary: Tau Kappa Epsilon is the largest social fraternity in the US, founded on January 10, 1899 at Illinois Wesleyan University. We stand not for wealth, rank or honor but for personal worth and character. TKE hopes to create better men for a better world.

name: Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Incorporated
summary: Kappa Alpha Psi is a social fraternity which prides itself in training for leadership and is geared toward achieving in every field of human endeavor.

name: Delta Gamma
summary: Delta Gamma offers to women of all ages a rich heritage, a continuity based on sound and tested principles of personal integrity, personal responsibility and intellectual honesty.

name: Gamma Phi Beta
summary: Gamma Phi Beta inspires the highest type of womanhood through education, service to humanity, and social means. Each member strives to live by and uphold the four core values we were founded upon: Love, Labor, Learning, and Loyalty.

name: Zeta Tau Alpha
summary: Zeta Tau Alpha was founded October 15, 1898 by nine women at the State Female Normal School in Farmville, Virginia. Only 15 years of age, these young women desired permanence to their friendships and hoped to perpetuate their sisterhood after college.

name: Cuong Nhu Martial Art Club
summary: Cuong Nhu, which means "hard soft" in Vietnamese, incorporates the best elements of several martial arts styles. It incorporates techniques from Shotokan Karate, Judo, Wing Chung, T'ai Chi Chuan, Akido, Boxing and Vovinam (A Vietnamese martial art).

name: Men's Ice Hockey Club at FSU
summary: The purpose of the Men's Ice Hockey Club at FSU is to promote excellence and increase awareness in all areas of FSU competition and to provide an outlet for men's ice hockey players at FSU to continue to participate in competitive ice hockey.

name: Roller Hockey Club at FSU
summary: We are a college roller hockey team, as members of the NCRHA and SECRHL conference. We travel to four tournaments in a single season, followed by both regional and national tournaments.

name: Weightlifting Club
summary: The Weightlifting Club is an organization focused on strength training.  Whether you've never been in a gym, or you've been pumping iron all your life, you will find resources and support to develop and improve upon safe, effective lifting techniques

name: AcaBelles
summary: Founded in 2000, AcaBelles is Florida State University's premiere soprano-alto a cappella ensemble.  We perform our own arrangements of popular music around campus and the community, travel on tours, and participate in international competitions.

name: Alpha Chi Sigma
summary: Following the original concept for a fraternity as an academic organization, we are the only professional chemistry fraternity. We bring together men and women pursuing a wide variety of chemistry-related careers.

name: American Association of Textile Chemists and Colorists
summary: Students mostly with majors in fields that will be in fashion, apparel, textiles and retail professions. AATCC hosts an on campus fashion show each semester, prepares members for their professional careers, and aids in personal and professional growth.

name: Anthropology Society at FSU
summary: The Anthropology Society at Florida State University (ASFSU) is a student-driven organization currently affiliated with the Lambda Alpha National Honor Society.

name: Women's Law Symposium
summary: WLS is dedicated to promoting the status of women in the legal community. WLS shall provide a forum for the discussion of issues concerning legal education and provide programs to advance and assist students throughout their law school career.

name: Women Student Union
summary: The Women Student Union is an identity-based group within Student Government whose overall mission is to unite women on the common purpose of individual self-growth.

name: Women in Business
summary: Women in Business at Florida State University aims to promote professional enrichment and education among FSU's most ambitious students.

name: Women in Accounting Organization at FSU
summary: The FSU College of Business chapter of Women in Accounting was organized during the Spring 2009 semester to address the unique challenges facing women entering the accounting field.

name: Art History Association
summary: The graduate Art History Association, or AHA, provides its members with a means for interacting with other students, faculty, and professionals in the field.

name: Asian American Student Union
summary: Established in 1996, the Asian American Student Union was created to better represent the Asian American community at Florida State University, spread awareness about Asian heritage and culture, and engage students in social events to build community.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
summary: The Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) is an affiliate under the Asian American Student Union (AASU) and is an organization representing Vietnamese culture at Florida State University!

name: Beta Alpha Psi - Beta Rho Chapter
summary: Beta Alpha Psi recognizes academic excellence and complements members' formal education by providing interaction among students, faculty, and professionals, while fostering lifelong growth, appreciation for service, and ethical conduct.

name: The Black Law Students Association, College Student Division at FSU
summary: The Pre-Law Division of the Black Law Students Association is an organization whose focus is to ensure that all students interested in law are being adequately informed, fostered, and empowered with the necessary tools to be successful in Law School.

name: The Black Student Union
summary: The Black Student Union seeks to develop unity among the Black students so that jointly and strongly they can express concerns and problems faced on campus. The Black Student Union attempts to create awareness to the total university community about

name: Florida State University Business Review
summary: The Business Review is a legal journal of the Florida State University College of Law and is designed to provide a scholarly forum for contemporary legal discourse and to address the issues and concerns transforming the business law community.

name: Circle K International
summary: Circle K International (CKI) is a collegiate service organization involved in a wide array of activities on a local and global scale. We work hand-in-hand with our partnering clubs under Kiwanis International.

name: Ecology and Evolution  Research Discussion Group at Florida State University
summary: This group provides a weekly forum for biology graduate students to engage in open and interactive discussion of their research ideas and accomplishments with their peers

name: Environmental Law Society
summary: The Environmental Law Society provides a forum to address key environmental law issues, pro bono and internship opportunities, and practitioner contacts to assist our members  transition into the professional network of environmental attorneys.

name: Economics Club at Florida State University
summary: The purpose of Econ Club is to further advance economic ideas, research, and opportunities for our members. We offer fascinating talks from our faculty and neighboring economic research centers to build a network of bright economists.

name: FAMU-FSU Engineers Without Borders
summary: FAMU-FSU Engineers Without Borders is a non-profit humanitarian organization established to partner with developing communities worldwide in order to improve their quality of life.

name: Financial Management Association at FSU
summary: The mission of the FMA is to broaden the common interests between academicians and practitioners, provide opportunities for professional interaction between and among academicians, practitioners, and students, and to promote finance education.

name: Florida Public Relations Association
summary: FPRA is for future public relations, digital marketing, and communications professionals. FPRA has bi-weekly meetings, events, and workshops where speakers discuss PR niches, industries, and companies.

name: Florida State University Law Review
summary: Providing a forum for contemporary legal discourse.

name: Graduate Women in STEM
summary: GWIS aims to provide a supportive and inclusive community that promotes professional development, leadership, and opportunities for women in STEM graduate programs during their time at FSU and beyond.

name: Health Law Society
summary:
We organize or participate in a variety of events on FSU campus and in Tallahassee, including round tables where law students and med students can discuss specific areas of health law and policy.
 
To foster discussion at the Flordia State

name: History Graduate Student Association
summary: HGSA is the representative organization for graduate students studying history. It acts as a liaison between faculty and graduate students, provides opportunities for professional development, and supports community through social events.

name: Institute of Industrial & Systems Engineers
summary: An Engineering student organization committed to developing professional, leadership, academic, and social networks that openly educates and promotes the Industrial Engineering profession to all students regardless of their disciplines.

name: Intellectual Property Law Society
summary: The Intellectual Property Law Society (IPLS) is an organization of law students interested in the field of intellectual property law.

name: Inter Residence Hall Council
summary: The mission of the Inter-Residence Hall Council, as an affiliate of both University Housing and SGA, is to engage students in the on-campus living experience through leadership development, community involvement, and peer advocacy.

name: International Law Students Association
summary: ILSA promotes the study of international legal topics. The organization promotes the growth of international law through its members and explores the international aspects of issues impacting our community.

name: Japanese Animation Club of Tallahassee
summary: Our purpose is to promote Japanese culture through exposing members to current trends in popular media, such as animation, graphic novels, video games, music and more. We intend to unite the diverse student body through a common interest.

name: Journal of Land Use and Environmental Law
summary: Advancing contemporary jurisprudence of the land, air, and waters.

name: The French Club at FSU
summary:
Bonjour à tous !!

We are group of undergraduate and graduate students of all levels of French who get together to practice French and celebrate Francophone culture. All students interested in French or francophone cultures are welcome!

name: Music Theory Society
summary: The goals of the Music Theory Society include fostering the development of all aspects of the discipline of music theory, including research and teaching.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
summary:
The NSBE mission is 

"To increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community."

name: Neuroscience Graduate Student Association
summary: The NGSA will provide an avenue for academic, professional, and social activities to the graduate students in the interdisciplinary Program in Neuroscience at Florida State University.

name: Philosophy Graduate Student Association
summary: PGSA is an association for all graduate students in the philosophy program at Florida State University, designed to foster interest in philosophy and philosophical issues within the academic community of Florida State University.

name: Political Science Graduate Student Association
summary: The Political Science Graduate Student Association is the official student organization of the Political Science graduate program at Florida State University.

name: Pride Student Union
summary: The Pride Student Union at FSU provides services and programs to address the needs of LGBTQ+ students and to increase the awareness of issues pertinent to them within the Florida State University and the greater Tallahassee community.

name: Progressive Black Men, Inc.
summary: Progressive Black Men is a brotherhood whose purpose is to reconstruct and redefine the image of the Black male and uplift people of African descent.

name: The Real Estate Society at FSU
summary: The Real Estate Society at FSU fosters career growth through social events, industry exposure, and networking with professionals via meetings, workshops, and corporate tours.

name: Relay For Life at Florida State University
summary:
Relay For Life unites students, faculty, and organizations across FSU in the fight against cancer. Benefiting the American Cancer Society, fundraising culminates in a 12-hour main event on campus in the spring. 
Celebrate, remember, and fight back! 🎗️💜

name: Society for Human Resource Management at FSU
summary: Society for Human Resource Management at FSU is an organization for students who want to build their business acumen through exposure to pertinent career and human resource topics.

name: Society for Musicology
summary: The purpose of the Society for Musicology is to foster and promote academic excellence among musicology students, acclimate new students to the musicology graduate program, create a positive learning environment for all students, and promote a sense of

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers at FAMU-FSU
summary:
SHPE is the leading social-technical organization whose primary function is to enhance and achieve the potential of Hispanics in engineering, math and science.

We will fulfill our mission by increasing educational opportunities and promoting diversity.

name: FAMU-FSU Society of Women Engineers
summary: SWE is a non-profit organization for people interested in engineering and supporting women in engineering. SWE is the driving force that establishes engineering as a highly desirable career aspiration for women.

name: Sociology Graduate Student Union
summary: SGSU is an organization that represents sociology graduate students in professional and departmental matters and promotes a supportive culture among graduate students.

name: Organization for Religion Graduate Students
summary: This organization provides an extra-departmental forum for the variety of students in the religion graduate program to come together. It seeks to promote a scholarly and professional atmosphere for students by organizing and hosting an annual symposium.

name: Student Interest Group In Neurology
summary: The purpose of SIGN is to educate and promote neurological medicine at FSU. This will be done primarily via meetings in which physicians, residents, and patients participate in discussions and provide information concerning the field of Neurology.

name: College of Business Student Leadership Council
summary: The Student Leadership Council will enrich the College of Business environment and represent the entire College of Business student body interest. The Student Leadership Council will be composed of highly motivated, discipline specific business students.

name: Federalist Society for Law and Public Policy Studies
summary: A non-partisan conservative and libertarian organization dedicated to fostering balanced and open debate about the fundamental principles of freedom, federalism, and judicial restraint.

name: Geriatrics Interest Group
summary: The Geriatrics Interest Group (GIG) of the Florida State University College of Medicine (FSU COM) is to educate and promote geriatric medicine at the Florida State University (FSU).

name: The Kudzu Review
summary: The Kudzu Review is FSU's undergraduate literary magazine established in 1988. Kudzu is a national publication that strives to provide undergraduate students with opportunities for publishing and literary experience in a full-scale editorial magazine.

name: The National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
summary: The mission of the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People is to ensure the political, educational, social and economic equality of rights of all persons and to eliminate racial hatred and racial discrimination.

name: The National Council of Negro Women
summary: NCNW is a voluntary, nonprofit membership organization with the mission to advance the opportunities and the quality of life for African American women, their families and their communities.

name: Transfer Student Organization at FSU Law
summary: Transfer Student Organization at Florida State College of Law is a program designed to facilitate a smooth transition for law students who transfer into FSU Law from a different law school.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Pre-Law Fraternity
summary: Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International is a professional law fraternity advancing integrity, compassion and courage through service to the student, the school, the profession and the community.

name: Pre-Optometry Club at FSU
summary: Pre-Optometry Club is for anyone who is interested in learning more about the visual system and a career in optometry! We provide students opportunities to volunteer in the field, speak to recruiters, and learn from the best local optometrists.

name: Slavic Student Association
summary:
The Slavic Student Association at Florida State University is an organization dedicated to promoting cultural exchange by celebrating Slavic culture and languages.

Open to all majors

name: Instructional Systems Student Association
summary: The Instructional Systems Student Association (ISSA) is an organization comprised of students, faculty, and alumni of the Instructional Systems and Learning Technologies program at the College of Education, Health, and Human Sciences at FSU.

name: Master of Science in Finance Association
summary: The Master of Science in Finance Association prepares MSF students to enter the job market, serve their community and university, and advance the reputation of the Florida State University College of Business.

name: Union Board
summary: The Union Board was established to provide students, faculty, staff, and guests with programs and services offered through the FSU Student Union. The Board represents the university community to ensure that facilities and amenities meet their needs.

name: We Take Education and Content Higher Club
summary: WeTEACH is the official social, academic, and service club of the FSU-Teach program.

name: Sigma Delta Pi Spanish Honors Society
summary: Sigma Delta Pi is the National Collegiate Hispanic Honor Society (La Sociedad Nacional Honoraria Hispnica). It was founded to encourage students to continue studying Spanish, allowing them to meet people with whom they shared commom interests,

name: Black Law Students Association, Graduate Chapter
summary: FSU BLSA helps integrate members into the College of Law community and the greater legal community through academic, mentoring, advocacy, networking, and community service opportunities in the Tallahassee area. We strive for excellence daily!

name: Student Alumni Association
summary: The Student Alumni Association (SAA) is a student organization that fosters spirit, tradition, and pride throughout the university. Our objective is to provide activities and programs to FSU students that can assist in their development as future alumni.

name: International Association of Emergency Managers - USA Student Chapter at FSU
summary: The International Association of Emergency Managers (IAEM) is the preeminent professional development organization for those studying or working in the field of Emergency Management.

name: Leadership, Education, And Research Network
summary: We are an affiliation of students drawn from across Florida State University's Educational Leadership & Policy program, dedicated to sharing research ideas and supporting each others' pursuits in the field of education.

name: American Marketing Association FSU
summary: The American Marketing Association is the world's largest and most comprehensive professional marketing society. Membership within AMA provides opportunities for professional development, marketing experience, and insights from current professionals.

name: Network of enlightened Women
summary: NeW is the nation's premier club for conservative university women. Started as a book club at the University of Virginia in 2004, NeW aims to cultivate a community of conservative women and expand intellectual diversity.

name: Turkish Student Association
summary: The purpose of TSA is to develop projects and activities to create awareness of Turkish culture, art, and history through talks, cultural events, and gatherings at FSU.

name: Light the Night at Florida State University
summary: This organization is dedicated to spreading awareness and advocating on behalf of those whose lives have been forever touched by blood cancers, such as leukemia, myeloma, and lymphoma. Our organization plans the annual Tallahassee Light The Night Walk.

name: College of Communication & Information Student Leadership Council
summary: The College of Communication & Information Student Leadership Council will serve as a liaison group between the student body, the Dean of the College and the alumni of the College of Communication and Information and the Florida State University.

name: The Film Club at FSU
summary: The Film Club at FSU is dedicated to providing a fun, professional organization for students seeking to create and discuss films of all types!

name: Sigma Phi Epsilon
summary: The Florida Epsilon Chapter of Sigma Phi Epsilon Fraternity at Florida State University.

name: Hugh O'Brian Leadership Student Association
summary: The Hugh O'Brian Leadership Student Association is a service-based organization whose goal is to unite HOBY alumni, friends, and faculty at Florida State University.

name: Association for Information Systems
summary: AIS is a professional organization dedicated to promoting better knowledge and understanding of information systems and technology. AIS aids students in career development via career building workshops, regular speaking engagements, and networking.

name: Lambda Pi Eta Communications and Information Honor Society at the Florida State University
summary: Lambda Pi Eta National Communication and Information Honor Society at Florida State University is designed to honor those students who excel in their communication programs. We are determined to distinguish and better prepare them for the future.

name: Business Law Society
summary: The Business Law Society (BLS) is dedicated to promoting academic and social interaction among students interested in the various aspects of business law.

name: Environmental Service Program
summary: ESP aims to engage students and community members with the environment around them through education, service, and advocacy. We focus our activities on the unique ecosystems and needs of the region surrounding Tallahassee.

name: Student Life Cinema
summary: Our movies are selected by an all-student committee and are free to all currently enrolled FSU students with FSUCard unless otherwise noted, and $5 for general admission.

name: ASLC Game Committee
summary: Game Committee creates and organizes all of the gaming events that take place at the Askew Student Life Center. They also select the games available for rental in the ASLC Cyber Café including console systems, board games, and PC library.

name: Spotlight Improv Club
summary: Spotlight Improv Club is FSU's one and only 100% improvised comedy club.

name: NSSLHA
summary: The National Student Speech Language Hearning Association (NSSLHA) is a pre-professional organization for undergraduate and graduate students interested in learning about the study of communication sciences and disorders.

name: Students for the Effective Communication of Science
summary: The purpose of this organization is to promote and facilitate interactions between the students enrolled in the Molecular Biophysics Graduate Program, as well as to promote wider understanding of the field of Biophysics.

name: Art Students' League of Florida State University
summary: A body of artists dedicated to building a community and learning professional art practices.

name: Women in Pre-Law Society
summary: This organization is a pre-law society geared towards women's advancement in the legal field.

name: Student Academy of Audiology
summary: The Student Academy of Audiology is a national student organization that works to advance the rights, interests, and welfare of students pursuing careers in audiology.

name: The Association for Criminal Justice
summary: The purpose of the Association of Criminal Justice is to expose law students to practical topics in the criminal justice system. The organization shall introduce students to criminal law matters from judicial, prosecutorial, & defense perspectives.

name: Division of Student Affairs
summary: The Division of Student Affairs collaborates with students, faculty, and staff to create welcoming, supportive and challenging environments that maximize opportunities for student learning and success.

name: New Student & Family Programs
summary: New Student & Family Programs coordinates a series of programs for both students and families designed to support the successful transition to Florida State University.

name: Senior Class Council
summary: Working to create unity amongst the graduating classes and pride amongst students own graduating class.

name: Student Council for Undergraduate Research and Creativity
summary: The Student Council for Undergraduate Research and Creativity (SCURC) is committed to promoting awareness about undergraduate research for students at Florida State University.

name: Student Government Association
summary: The mission of the Student Government Association (SGA), is to provide FSU students with representation, services and advocacy within the university structure.

name: Student Government Association Accounting
summary: The SGA staff supports the student leaders in Student Government atFSU. We provide an educational atmosphere in which the student leaders can participate in the active and important role that SGA has in making effective changes in student policy.

name: Higher Education Student Association
summary: HESA serves as the collective voice and represents the needs of all students associated with the Higher Education program.

name: North Florida Chapter of the American Meteorological Society/ National Weather Association
summary: We are a local chapter of the American Meteorological Society and National Weather Association designed to serve the local Tallahassee community and the meteorological community in North Florida through outreach and social programs.

name: SG&A Student Publications
summary: We are a team of student creatives providing free color printing, graphic design, and photography services for Recognized Student Organizations on campus. We're an affiliated project of Student Government Association. Come visit us in Thagard 425!

name: WVFS Tallahassee 89.7FM (V89)
summary: Mission: To provide diversity in programming to the Florida State campus and Tallahassee community while acting as a top-notch training facility for students and community members interested in gaining experience in radio station operations.

name: Junior Class Council at Florida State University
summary: A council to create unity and programming for the junior class.

name: Center for Global Engagement
summary: The Mission of the Center for Global Engagement is to facilitate international diversity and foster global understanding and awareness within the FSU community.

name: Phi Eta Sigma National Honor Society
summary: Phi Eta Sigma is the oldest and largest national first-year honor society. Our goal is to encourage and reward academic excellence among first-year students in institutions of higher learning.

name: Council for Exceptional Children
summary: Our student chapter of CEC aims to advance the success of individuals with exceptionalities through education, advocacy, service, and professional development.

name: Neuroscience Undergraduate Student Association
summary: NUSA is an undergraduate organization for students interested in neuroscience.  Its goals are to foster knowledge towards the field of neuroscience to its members and provide outreach programs to the community relating to the subject.

name: Freshman Leadership Institute
summary: The Freshman Leadership Institute is an Executive Project through SGA which aims to provide first year students with the tools to become active student leaders on campus while providing support as students adjust to the FSU's campus and culture

name: Psi Chi International Honor Society in Psychology
summary: Psi Chi is an international honor society whose purpose shall be to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship of the individual members in all fields, particularly in psychology, and to advance the science of psychology.

name: The Jim Moran Institute for Global Entrepreneurship
summary: The Jim Moran Institute for Global Entrepreneurship

name: College of Social Sciences and Public Policy Student Leadership Council
summary: The College of Social Sciences and Public Policy Student Leadership Council serves as a liaison group between the College's student body and the Dean of the College to provide programming, events, and opportunities to all students of the college and FSU.

name: The Cell and Molecular Biology Graduate Student Association
summary: The Cell and Molecular Biology Graduate Association aims to foster solidarity between students in the Cell and Molecular Biology graduate program within the Department of Biological Sciences at Florida State University.

name: Bangladeshi Student Association
summary: This group is to protect, facilitate, improve and serve the interests of students of Bangladeshi descent.

name: Gamma Iota Sigma, Iota Chapter
summary: The purpose of this fraternity is to provide students who are interested in insurance and actuarial science the opportunity to interact with industry professionals and build connections with their fellow students.

name: Club Management Association of America at FSU
summary: CMAA, or Club Managers Association of America, is a professional organization compromised of public and private club managers from around the U.S. As a student chapter we are able to tap into their resources for education, training, and networking.

name: Theta Tau Professional Engineering Fraternity
summary: Theta Tau is the oldest and largest Fraternity for Engineers, with chapters across the country, and members around the world. Our mission is to develop ENGINEERING LEADERS for Service, Profession, and Brotherhood.

name: Multicultural Marketing Student Association
summary: MMSA empowers its diverse members with valuable experiences, strong networks, essential tools, and key resources to support their professional and personal growth

name: Hispanic Graduate Student Association
summary: HiGSA is a diverse organization aimed at promoting the Hispanic culture at FSU and serving as a network support for new and current graduate students realizing the full potential of diversity and inclusion. The organization is constantly working towards

name: Center for Academic Retention and Enhancement
summary: CARE provides preparation, orientation, and academic support programming for students who are among the first in their family to attend college and may face unique challenges in college because of educational or economic circumstances.

name: Fundamentals of Observational Research and Career Experience
summary: Shadowing in Tallahassee can be competitive. FORCE is here to help dedicated, hardworking, diligent students find a health professional, and mentorship to well-round the individual before they start their future careers.

name: The Consulting Group at Florida State University
summary: The Consulting Group at Florida State University is a student-run firm leveraging top business students' insights to provide innovative, data-driven solutions to businesses nationwide. 2025 applications are live on our LinkTree: https://linktr.ee/cgatfsu

name: International Studies Graduate Association
summary: A graduate student organization for International Affairs, Russian and Eastern European Studies, and Asian Studies Programs.

name: National Association for Music Education Collegiate of Florida State University
summary: NAfME Collegiate of FSU gives music education students a plethora of opportunities to gain knowledge about their future intended profession that they would otherwise not receive in the normal classroom setting.

name: Who We Play For Chapter at Florida State University
summary: We fight sudden cardiac arrest and heart health through heart screenings in the local area. Our Movement is to inspire you with our story of overcoming adversity, while providing you the platform to make an impact for something greater than yourself!

name: Physics Graduate Student Association
summary: The main objective of this organization is to represent the needs, interests and internal concerns of Florida State physics graduate students to faculty and administration.

name: Cardiology Interest Group
summary: The purpose of the Cardiology Interest Group is to promote an interest and understanding of cardiac medicine at Florida State University College of Medicine.

name: Florida Association of Environmental Professionals
summary: FAEP at FSU helps students interested in working in the environmental field by providing networking opportunities & career advice from professionals in the environmental field. This includes internship opportunities & potential jobs after graduation.

name: Big Sister Little Sister at Florida State University
summary: The purpose of BSLS is promote and advocate for the uplift of young women, especially all female incoming/current freshman of Florida State University.

name: The School of Teacher Education Graduate Student Association
summary: The School of Teacher ed has many graduate tracks from Early Childhood Education to Math Education to Special Education. This group seeks to develop a supportive community in which we productively share our ideas, feedback, and learn from each other.

name: Beta Beta Beta Biological Honor Society
summary: Beta Beta Beta is a nationally recognized honor society that focuses on promoting research and giving students the opportunity to participate in biologically relevant events. We also frequently host panels and community service events for our members.

name: Human Development and Family Science Club
summary: The Purpose of the Human Development and Family Sciences Club is to provide the student body of FSU the opportunity to enhance their knowledge of lifespan development, and to provide service and opportunities that support children, adults, and families.

name: Criminology and Criminal Justice Student Leadership Council
summary: The CCJ Student Leadership Council serves as a liaison between the College and the students within the college. Together, we work to enhance the Criminology and Criminal Justice program here at FSU, and bring more opportunities to FSU.

name: Center for the Advancement of Human Rights
summary: The mission of CAHR is to facilitate interdisciplinary human rights courses, research, and events as well as engage in direct community and global human rights advocacy.

name: Residential Scholars Program
summary: To be added.

name: InterProfessional Allies
summary: The IPA will promote involvement of students and professionals from various disciplines to foster the FSU mission of excellence in teaching, research, creative endeavors, and service with an innovative perspective of interprofessional collaboration.

name: ChemiNoles Graduate Student Association
summary: This organization is for Chemistry Graduate Students looking to develop professionally, network in and out of the department, and take part in outreach within the city of Tallahassee as well as globally.

name: Social Spear
summary: Social Spear is an organization for all majors that are interested in the marketing industry. We aim to teach members how to market themselves as an individual through guest speakers, workshops, and marketing games.

name: White Mouse Productions at FSU
summary: White Mouse Productions creates transformative theater for social change. Through workshops, innovative productions, and powerful performances, we inspire action and foster community engagement in Tallahassee and beyond.

name: Graduate Literature Organization
summary: The Graduate Literature Organization seeks to 1) facilitate cohesion among graduate English literature students, 2) advocate on behalf of literature interests, 3) organize an annual symposium/conference, and 4) access further professionalization training

name: Sigma Alpha Iota
summary: Sigma Alpha Iota is an organization which promotes interaction among those who share a commitment to music. Women of SAI are active in all areas of campus music and campus life, working closely with faculty, administration, campus and community groups

name: Association for Women in Mathematics Student Chapter at Florida State University
summary: The AWM is a student organization that hopes to encourage women and other minorities to study and to have active careers in the mathematical sciences, and to promote equal opportunity and the equal treatment of all people in mathematics.

name: Power of We
summary: Power of WE is a student-led, University initiative that seeks to inspire a campus culture of innovation and collaboration by challenging echo chambers, building shared spaces, and bridging civic divides.

name: Nu Rho Psi - Beta of Florida Chapter
summary: Nu Rho Psi is the National Honor Society in Neuroscience, founded in 2006 by the Faculty for Undergraduate Neuroscience. Membership is by application and is open to undergraduates, graduates, and alumni of neuroscience.

name: Association of Latino Professionals For America at Florida State University
summary: ALPFA is a national, not-for-profit professional association that creates opportunities, adds value, and builds relationships for its members, the community and its business partners while expanding Latino Leadership in the global workforce.

name: Graduate Researchers of Geography
summary: This organization serves as a creative and supportive community for graduate students in the Department of Geography.

name: The Student Academy of The American Academy of Physician Assistants at FSU
summary: The Student Academy of The American Academy of Physician Assistants at FSU will represent the PA Program of the College of Medicine to the community at large. SAAAPA-FSU will participate and advocate for students at both the state and federal level.

name: Garnet and Gold Investment Club
summary: A financial literacy organization who's goal is to inform students on how to manage their money, and the different ways that they can accumulate wealth.

name: Gift of Life at FSU
summary: Gift of Life at FSU organizes events to register new potential donors in the bone marrow donor registry and raise awareness for the bone marrow donation process.

name: Society of Undergraduate Mathematics Students
summary: The mission of SUMS is to promote a greater sense of mathematical community within Florida State University and provide resources to aid in student success, especially as it relates to mathematics. Follow us on instagram for updates! @fsu.sums

name: Women Wednesday's Association at FSU
summary: Women Wednesday's Association at FSU is a social, mentoring organization that helps women with life during college and the transition after graduation by promoting campus resources.

name: Florida Restaurant and Lodging Association at Florida State
summary: The FSU chapter of the Florida Restaurant and Lodging Association aims to help hospitality students enter into the various sectors of the industry through guest speakers, site visits, and networking opportunities.

name: Sales Club at FSU
summary: Professional student organization dedicated to guide Business students toward a Sales career in different industries by bringing guest speakers who are leaders in their industries.

name: Student Florida Education Association
summary: We are a committed group of aspiring educators who believe preparing ourselves to be the best possible version we can become. The liability is just an added bonus to your membership. We offer professional development, resources, state and national confer

name: Friday Night Live
summary: Friday Night Live is FSU's only comedy variety show, featuring live sketches, digital shorts, news, and music. We follow the format of NBC's Saturday Night Live, including Weekend Update, guest hosts and musical acts.

name: Spanish Graduate Student Organization
summary: This organization was created to aid graduate students interested in Spanish language instruction and research through academic and cultural events, including guest speakers and workshops.

name: Department of Urban and Regional Planning Student Association
summary: This organization has been formed for the purpose of professional development, community outreach, networking, academic productivity, and mentorship of students pursuing careers in Urban and Regional Planning.

name: Thrift Club at FSU
summary:
Thrift Club is a community of students at FSU who share a love for secondhand shopping and all things vintage.
At our core we support sustainability, entrepreneurship, and community involvement!

name: Alliance for Black Women
summary: Alliance for Black Women is an organization that strives to promote the holistic development of Black women through empowerment, sisterhood, and service.

name: Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Association
summary: MEGSA is an organization that aims to bring together graduate students from different focuses in mechanical engineering and foster interdisciplinary interest and discussion within the academic community, and represent FSU in the ME community at large.

name: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers at FAMU/FSU
summary: The Institute of Electrical and Electronic Engineers (IEEE) is a global association and organization of professionals working toward the development, implementation and maintenance of technology-centered products and services.

name: Students for Life at Florida State University
summary: Students for Life exists to support students in unplanned pregnancies and to stand for the humanity of all persons, preborn and born.

name: Strategies for Ecology Education Diversity and Sustainability FSU
summary: SEEDS aims to diversify the field of ecology to include underrepresented populations at all education levels.

name: Black Men in Medicine Inc.
summary: Black Men in Medicine is an organization that serves to uplift black males who have an interest in medicine and to provide them with knowledge and experiences in professional healthcare settings.

name: The Society of Black Female Future Attorneys
summary: The Society of Black Female Future Attorneys is a pre-law society designed to support Black and minority female students as they pursue their career paths toward the law field at FSU.

name: Women in Government at FSU
summary: Empower, embrace, and create long lasting relationships amongst like-minded women who are interested in government. WIG will help develop young women into outstanding leaders through internship opportunities, networking, and group discussions

name: Federal Bar Association at FSU
summary: The Federal Bar Association at FSU will serve as a law student chapter of the larger Federal Bar Association national organization.

name: The Trombone Studio at FSU
summary: This organization is made up of members of the FSU Trombone Studio at the FSU College of music, as well as non majors with an interest in trombone.

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers at FSU
summary: The mission of SASE is to prepare Asian heritage scientists and engineers for success in the global business world, celebrate diversity on campus, and provide opportunities for members to make  contributions to their local communities.

name: Themed Entertainment Association at Florida State
summary: The NextGen Initiative of the Themed Entertainment Association (TEA) helps students and recent graduates find their way into the themed entertainment/visitor attractions industry. Our initiative at Florida State was founded in Fall 2020.

name: Undergraduate Law Review at FSU
summary: The Undergraduate Law Review at Florida State University is comprised of a team of dedicated undergraduate writers and editors. We are a pre-law organization that is entirely student-run, and we publish long- and short-form legal writing on our website.

name: Epilepsy Foundation of Florida at FSU
summary: Our mission is to raise awareness about the challenges of living with epilepsy and to promote research on therapies to stop seizures, find cures, and save lives.

name: The Museum Society at Florida State
summary: We aim to promote dialogue surrounding museum experiences and knowledge across all disciplines. The organization will act as a resource to students to gather a community of like-minded individuals who both enjoy visiting and working in museums.

name: ASLC Special Events Committee
summary: The SLC Special Events Committee...

name: Collegiate Entrepreneurs Organization
summary: We are the FSU chapter of a larger national organization dedicated to facilitating college students in the pursuit of their entrepreneurial endeavors through networking events and hands-on trainings with various successful entrepreneurs.

name: Hispanic Law Students Association
summary: The purpose of HLSA is to provide opportunities for social, academic and professional development for students interested in law at Florida State University. Our goal is to empower all students within the university as well as the community at large.

name: The Studio Orchestra Society at FSU
summary: The Studio Orchestra Society at FSU offers student arrangers, composers, and conductors the opportunity perform music from film, video games, and other entertainment media for the student body and Tallahassee community.

name: American Hellenic Educational Progressive Association Scholars at Florida State University
summary: To promote the ancient Hellenic ideals of education, philanthropy, civic responsibility, integrity, and family and individual excellence through celebration of heritage, service, and brotherhood.

name: Jiu Jitsu Club
summary: Jiu Jitsu is a martial art that focuses on self-defense and grappling. Our goal is to raise our student's confidence while having fun. We guarantee a safe and positive training environment. For roster spots reach out on instagram. See you on the mat!

name: Masters in Applied American Politics and Policy Association
summary: The MAAPP Association is designed for those who seek active careers in a number of political fields: Campaigns, Fundraising, Political Communications, Lobbying, Legislative Affairs, etc. All members of the MAAPP program and those interested are welcome!

name: Forward at FSU
summary: Building a positive & enriching culture among student leaders who seek to serve our FSU community!

name: The Women’s Network FSU
summary: TWN is a collegiate women’s networking organization that focuses on empowering and connecting high-achieving women to each other and to leaders across many industries, as well as celebrating ambition.

name: POLY/PMSE Student Chapter at FAMU-FSU
summary: A professional student run organization affiliated with American Chemical Society for students in a polymer chemistry field. POLY/PMSE is a joint organization between FAMU and FSU.

name: Cryptocurrency Organization for Young Professionals
summary: Our organization is dedicated to discussing cryptocurrency topics, networking with those familiar/new to the cryptocurrency industry, and learning about exciting cryptocurrency projects. Regardless of experience, everyone is welcome.

name: Students Demand Action at Florida State University
summary: Students Demand Action organization aims to raise awareness about gun safety. It is a student-led grassroots organization that pushes sensible gun legislation and shifting gun culture in America.

name: Stitch Fashion and Design Club
summary: The singular Fashion & Design club at Florida State University, Stitch teaches any curious individuals in Tallahassee about the various significances of fashion and its unique position as an art form in today's world.

name: School of Teacher Education Graduate Student Association
summary: The School of Teacher Education Graduate Student Association (STEGSA) aims to create a supportive community in which we share our ideas, get feedback, and learn from each other.

name: Script2Screen
summary: Script2Screen is solely dedicated to making screenwriting accessible and fun for students. If you're interested in movies, television, podcasts, and other forms of media, this is the club for you!

name: National Organization for the Professional Advancement of Black Chemists and Chemical Engineers
summary: The mission of the NOBCChE is to build an eminent cadre of successful diverse global leaders in STEM and advance their professional endeavors by adding value to their academic, development, leadership, and philanthropic endeavors.

name: Silver Wings
summary: Silver Wings (SW) is a national, co-ed, professional organization dedicated to creating proactive, knowledgeable, and effective civic leaders through community service and education about national defense.

name: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space at FAMU and FSU
summary: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space at FAMU-FSU is an organization that seeks to unite people from all different disciplines and backgrounds on their shared interest in space through interdisciplinary projects and educational outreach.

name: Campus Emergency Response Volunteers
summary: Campus Emergency Response Volunteers (CERV) assists with some emergency-related activities on campus, such as shelter operations, distributing goods throughout campus, or providing basic first aid. Any student, faculty, or staff member may join.

name: SLC Video Production Committee
summary: We are the committee in charge of Filming, Pre /Post Production and  Editing for all things ASLC. If you are interested in learning to edit, acting in videos or filming projects please join us!

name: Technology & Entrepreneurship Club
summary: TEC brings education, networking opportunities, and speakers from the worlds of technology and entrepreneurship to FSU law students.

name: DevLUp at FSU
summary: A Game Development Club at FSU.

name: African Graduate Students Association
summary: The African Graduate Students Association (AGSA) at Florida State University promotes African cultures and traditions on campus, facilitates mutual relationships among students of African descent, and advocates for their needs on campus.

name: STEMental Health Association
summary: The STEMental Health Association is a student-led organization on campus focusing on destigmatizing mental health conversations within the STEM field. Our aim is to create a supportive, educational, and safe space.

name: Step-by-Step Club at Florida State University
summary: The Step-by-Step Club at Florida State University is here to create a better environment and improve the lives of those less fortunate, including children in need and the homeless population within Tallahassee, by collecting and donating needed items.

name: The Nineteen Ten Honorary
summary: The Nineteen Ten Honorary serves to recognize women and people who support women who have shown dedication to ambition, compassion, and innovation. We strive to create an empowering community to inspire the next generation of women leaders.

name: The Rosenstrasse Foundation at FSU
summary: The Rosenstrasse Foundation was established to educate the public about the Rosenstrasse Protest, as well as its impact and significance, honoring the memory of the protesters involved.

name: Private Equity & Venture Capital Club at FSU
summary: The Private Equity & Venture Capital Club at FSU is committed to providing its members with career and educational opportunities in private equity and venture capital. We provide a platform for students interested in private equity and venture capital.

name: Psychology Club at FSU
summary: Psychology Club at FSU is a registered student organization focused on social, professional, and academic support of the undergraduate students at Florida State University interested in Psychology and related fields of study.

name: GIVE Volunteers at Florida State University
summary: Our purpose is to inspire growth, empower the marginalized, and encourage sustainable change worldwide through promoting sustainable volunteer efforts, globally-minded initiatives, environmentally-conscious lifestyles, and adventurous activities.

name: Perfect Pair Chapter at FSU
summary: This program creates 1-on-1 connections between senior citizens & college students. Pairs are based on similar hobbies & interests. Through laughs, conversation, & activities, our members have the opportunity to foster meaningful, lasting relationships.

name: Society of Physics Students at Florida State University
summary: SPS is a professional association for students interested in physics. Our goals are to encourage and assist students interested in physics, help facilitate professional development among students, and promote public interest in physics.

name: Spoonbill Society: Birding Club at FSU
summary: The mission of this club is to encourage enthusiasm about birding and to foster curiosity about the natural world.

name: Crochet Society at FSU
summary:
Crochet Society offers a learning experience for beginners to experts in crochet and more.
https://www.fsucrochetsociety.com/home

name: The Linguistics Organization
summary: The Linguistics Organization (LINGO) strives to create a collaborative, student-centered club that creates a community for aspiring linguists.

summary: The Child Psychology club aims to provide interested students with more experience in Child Psychology and contributing fields of study by promoting education and advice supporting the development of children and adolescents!

name: The Sunshine Society at FSU
summary: We are a nonpartisan student research group dedicated to shining a light on Florida politics. We provide students with nonpartisan research on legislative policies, allowing them to better educate themselves and make informed political decisions.

name: Art Society at FSU
summary: TBD

summary: SAI info here!

summary: This platform aims to be a hub for networking, workshops, research support, and professional development opportunities, empowering engineers to thrive both academically and professionally, helping students find their future goal for industry or academia.

summary: The purpose of this Organization is to provide anesthesia care-related exposure, engagement, and service opportunities to Florida State University undergraduate students intending to pursue a Master of Science in Anesthesia graduate degree.

summary: Soul Sister is a local nonprofit organization that strives to create constant impact through community service, mentorship, professional development, and higher education by redefining and rebuilding the outlook on African American women and youth.

summary: The Southeast Review, established in 1979 as Sundog, is a national literary magazine housed in the English department at Florida State University, edited and managed by graduate students.

summary: Women in Finance & Economics at FSU is a place to provide opportunities and empower women in the world of Finance and Economics.

summary: The purpose of our student association is to provide Ecuadorian students at FSU with a place like home where they can immerse themselves in our culture, and history, and share these with anyone interested in learning more about Ecuador.

summary: The Doctoral Business Student Association (DBSA) brings doctoral students in business together for academic and social purposes.

summary: We aim to provide a welcoming community for individuals passionate about the brain and its mysteries, creating an environment where members can come together to meet like-minded peers, engage in study sessions, and participate in hands-on experiments.

summary: The Freshman Class Council serves the Freshman Class at Florida State University. The Freshman Class Council hosts programming dedicated to development for students here at Florida State University.

name: Bonsai Club at FSU
summary: The bonsai club is a place where people talk about trees.

name: Group for Artificial Intelligence Navigation
summary: GAIN at FSU

summary: Business Students from Abroad is a welcoming community for international students pursuing business degrees at FSU. Members engage in the academic and social experience of our club through various activities, workshops, and events.

summary: Her University's mission towards sisterhood and pushing everyone to become their best HER. Whether that be spiritually,emotionally, physically, financially, or professionally, they are striving towards creating a community on campus that allows everyone.

summary: The Torch Society (formerly the Academic Recruitment Organization) is dedicated to furthering the academic and professional success of students at FSU.

summary: RTSA provides a community for students studying Recreation and Tourism Management. Students will have the opportunity to connect with industry professionals and attend social, professional, and volunteering events.

summary: The mission of the Lending Leaders Association at FSU is to prepare students and bridge the gap between academic learning and real-world experience. We plan on doing this by offering workshops, networking events, and mentorship opportunities from industr

summary: We are students who strive to make student's pre-professional dreams a reality by informing them of free resources we offer for tests like MCAT, LSAT, GRE, etc. We also partner with clubs/orgs who are pre med/pre law to further express what we have!

summary: The Civic Engagement Board (CEB) is an affiliated project of the College of Social Sciences and Public Policy designed to engage and educate the campus community in the critical institutions within our local, state, and national political systems.

summary: The Business Living-Learning Community is a community of non-formally admitted business majors, living together in Azalea Hall, taking courses together as they navigate their way into their major.

summary: We are Digital Media Production majors wanting to create a community among our major with a big/little program, media production for reels/portfolios not associated with DMP classes, and community events.

summary: We were founded on ensuring career and leadership success for women in all fields, regardless of their backgrounds and beliefs. Women's Progress is committed to being an advocate for women all across the Florida State campus and beyond.

name: The International Lampoon Review at Florida State University
summary: The Lampoon is a performance comedy club, focusing on everyday news and social issues. Satire is the name, and Satire is the game.

summary: Young Americans for Freedom (YAF) is a conservative youth activism organization that was founded in 1960 as a coalition between traditional conservatives and libertarians on American college campuses.

summary: The SLC will be assisting with recruitment networking events, inspire college students to seek opportunity through the Dedman College of Hospitality, and focus on retention of the students involved in our current programs.

summary: The ALA chapter at Florida State University is devoted to supporting all students as they explore the worlds of Information, and Information Technology. Our chapter actively promotes diversity, equity, and inclusion.

summary: YDSA at FSU's mission is to educate and organize students to play a helpful and principled role in the movement for social and economic justice.

name: Trinidadians for Science and Art Club
summary: There are barely any people from Trinidad and Tobago on campus. So I thought about making a club. The point of TT is not just to inform people about Trinidad and Tobago, but also to allow people to express their interests in science and art.

name: Women in IT/ICT Sharing Experiences
summary: The organization is focused on students in the Information Technology (IT) and the Information, Communication, and Technology (ICT) programs, however, we welcome students from all majors.

name: Psychology Association of Asian Americans
summary: Focused on strengthening diversity and supporting the Asian American community in the field of psychology at FSU.

name: The Society of Black Women in Education
summary: The Society of Black Women in Education (SBWE) empowers Black women pursuing careers in education through mentorship, advocacy, and professional development. We foster community, leadership, and equity in education. Join us and make an impact!

name: Seminole Blockchain Society
summary: The Seminole Blockchain Society (SBS) empowers FSU students to explore Web 3.0 technologies, including blockchain, DeFi, and NFTs. Through workshops, networking, and potential partnerships with organizations like Ripple, SBS fosters innovation& education

name: Akofena Kinship of Big Brother Little Brother Mentoring Program, Inc
summary: Big Brother Little Brother Mentoring Program, Inc. is dedicated to helping high school seniors have a smooth transition into college life by matching these students with upperclassmen who have the knowledge and experience to help!

name: CompNeuroSociety at FSU
summary: We established CompNeuroSociety at FSU in Spring '25. Our mission is to create a peer-led learning community, to increase access for FSU students who are interested in Computational Neuroscience, through a peer-led learning community.

name: Digital Media Club at FSU
summary: We're open to any major and anyone who has a passion for photography and design! We will host photography excursions, workshops for design software (Adobe Products, Canva, etc.), fun competitions, socials, guest speakers, and more!

name: Sociology Collective
summary: The Sociology Collective is a mentorship network and social club for our undergraduate sociology majors and minors. The programming will focus on how to get where you want to go with sociology and making connections within the major.

name: Campus Cats Club at FSU
summary: The goal of this organization is to connect students with FSU Facilities and local Trap Neuter Vaccinate Return groups to care for stray cat populations on FSU's campus.

name: National Lawyers Guild at FSU
summary: The NLG is dedicated to the need for basic change in the structure of our political and economic system. Our aim is to bring together all those who recognize the importance of safeguarding and extending rights.

name: Business and STEM Association at FSU
summary: The Business & STEM Association (BSA) at FSU unites students with diverse backgrounds to foster innovation, service, and real-world impact through learning, networking, competitions, and startup projects.

name: AI in Business at Florida State University
summary: The purpose of AIIB is to educate, empower, and support students in understanding the evolving role of artificial intelligence in the business sector. AIIB aims to bridge the gap between technological advancements in AI and their practical applications.

name: Flame: Law in Fashion, Art, Media, and Entertainment
summary: FLAME is a student organization that explores how the law applies to fashion, art, media, entertainment, and other related fields, making it a great opportunity for anyone with an interest in these areas to learn more.

name: The Eventing Team at FSU
summary: The Eventing Team at FSU aims to foster a supportive community of 3-day eventers. We compete under the USEA Intercollegiate Eventing Program and attend various challenges around Area lll.

name: The ColorStack Chapter at FSU
summary: ColorStack @ FSU is dedicated to building community, boosting representation, and helping Black and Latinx computer science students thrive, both on campus and in their future careers.

name: Rare Ribbon Association
summary: We empower cancer survivors with support and education, bringing people together through events and speakers to enhance research and survivorship.

name: Fulbright Students Association
summary: Fulbright Students Association (FSA) is an organization promoting interdisciplinary collaboration, cultural exchange, and professional development to foster mutual understanding and peaceful relations among Fulbright students at FSU.

name: The Eggplant at FSU
summary: The Eggplant is the leading satirical news publication on campus with prominent platforms across all social media, including its own website. Our team of writers meets weekly to produce articles and "newsworthy" stories from FSU, Tallahassee, and beyond.

name: Bricks and Bridges Club
summary: Bricks and Bridges Club has the goal to bring FSU students and neurodivergent children in Tallahassee together to build LEGO creations, share laughter, and celebrate each child’s unique strengths while creating a space where everyone feels connected.

name: Health Informatics Student Association
summary: The FSU Health Informatics Student Association (HISA) is a student-led organization dedicated to exploring the intersection of healthcare, technology, and innovation. Our mission is to empower students to shape the future of health informatics through co

name: The Finance Society
summary: The Finance Society is a pre-professional student organization designed to expose and prepare students for various careers in finance through weekly workshops, corporate tours, and guest speakers.

name: Geographical Society at FSU
summary: Supporting the academic, professional, and social growth of geography students at Florida State University.

name: Innovation Club at FSU
summary: The Innovation Club seeks to help students look at everyday problems and find solutions through collaboration, experimentation, and innovation. Our goal is to bring innovation to FSU through workshops, guest speakers, events, and competitions.

name: Cyber Org at FSU
summary: Our mission is to foster a community of awareness, education, and collaboration in cybersecurity at FSU and the surrounding communities. Through workshops, seminars, and hands-on experiences, we aim to raise awareness on cyber threats.

name: Incubate Debate at FSU
summary: Incubate Debate challenges Seminoles to debate. We do this by providing free, open access debates and workshops to discuss timely and timeless issues relating to everyday politics and public discourse. We present these debates in a civil manner for all.

name: Justice-Impacted Student Organization
summary: The Justice-Impacted Student Organization (JISO) empowers students with lived-experience in the criminal legal system to transform resilience into leadership, and education into opportunity. Students who wish to join as "Advocates" are encouraged.

name: Cabales Serrada Escrima Club
summary: This club will teach you how to use everyday objects to help defend yourself. We will practice with rattan sticks to learn techniques, blend this style with other martial arts, and engage in sparring.

name: Mathematics Graduate Student Council at FSU
summary: We serve as the liaison between graduate students and the Math Department. The GSC is a resource for faculty to learn student issues and helps current, incoming, and potential students transition smoothly into our programs.

name: Patient Advocacy Association
summary: The Patient Advocacy Association empowers students to support patients and families in the local community through education, outreach, and service that promote healthcare access, health literacy, and compassion-driven advocacy.

name: Secret to Success at FSU
summary: Secret to Success (S2S) is a student-led organization committed to empowering high school students as they prepare for life after graduation.

name: Albums in Review at FSU
summary: Albums in Review at FSU is a student organization that brings music lovers together to experience and discuss albums in a similar way to a book club, focusing on novels. Members listen to and analyze a selected album, exploring themes and artistry.

name: Lex et Lux Society
summary: Lex et Lux shows all parts of the law; academia, professional opportunities in- and out-of-country, research, law school experience, and more. We dive into 1L experiences, legal and legal adjacent careers, and things many pre law students don't consider.

name: Students for Progressive victory at FSU
summary:
Progressive Victory at FSU
is a student-led
organization dedicated to
advancing progressive
policy through collective
action. We empower young
voices on campus to hold
leaders accountable and
work toward a
progressive future.

summary: Student-led group partnering with Global Brigades and underserved communities to design sustainable water, sanitation, and infrastructure projects. Hands-on engineering, fundraising, travel, and leadership development.

name: Consumer Advocates Society
summary: The Consumer Advocates Society aims to promote, educate, and advocate for consumer rights at the state and federal levels. This organization aims to educate the FSU community about consumer rights in Florida and advocate for stronger protections.

name: Tabletop Gaming Club at FSU
summary: We are an organization that aims to foster a community built around tabletop games and related hobbies. We hope to attract open-minded students who are willing to learn new gaming systems and connect with others.

name: Bridge for Civic Leadership Council
summary: Bridge for Civic Leadership Council is an organization dedicated to bridging the gap between academic learning and professional application. This will be done through our semesterly Civic Project, mentorship, workshops, and community engagement.

name: Public Policy Journal at FSU
summary: The Public Policy Journal at FSU is intended to foster research excellence among those in the university with interests in international, national, and state issues. It will provide an undergraduate alternative to students seeking to publish in journals.

name: Students For Bead the Change
summary: The purpose of this club is to create handmade beaded bracelets that spread joy and encouragement by donating them to women’s shelters, children’s hospitals and other organizations in need.

name: Hispanic/Latine Pre-Law Society
summary: This is a Hispanic/Latine led organization focusing on increasing representation in the legal profession. We hope to help all undergraduate Latine students pursue this profession in the form of meetings, group speakers, workshops, and more.

name: Association of Claws and Clauses

name: Student Innovator's Beyond Borders
summary:
The Sustainability In Technology Club (SIT) empowers students to develop technology-driven solutions to worldwide environmental issues. 

Note: Originally known as Student Innovators Beyond Borders

name: Pi Sigma Alpha - The National Political Science Honors Society
summary: Pi Sigma Alpha is an honors society specifically for political science students or related social science majors. The organization is intended to foster community, provide career development opportunities, and highlight student achievements.

name: Future Scholars Society
summary: Working with Leon County School Districts, we will connect students who wish to tutor with teachers to earn community service hours. This way, teachers can plan lessons that need additional help. In the classroom, I go tofor a 20% bump in state test scor

name: Peace protection project
summary: We are a community driven nonprofit organization providing the next generation of teenagers and adults with opportunities to serve marginalized communities, at risk youth, and promote environmental conservation.

name: Robotics Club at FSU
summary: A club for passionate  students to get involved in robotics and have hands-on experience working with electronics, practical applications of robots, programming, and engineering in a team.

name: Mu Epsilon Delta at FSU
summary: Mu Epsilon Delta is a national co-educational, pre-health professional fraternity whose mission is to prepare members to succeed in pre-health careers through the development of scholarship, fraternity, and service.
